items.title: The US border runs straight through the World Cup
items.summary: The tournament was sold as a symbol of unity, but Trump’s border politics have turned it into a spectacle of exclusion.

items.title: US orders Anthropic to disable AI models for all foreign nationals
items.summary: The company said it received ⁠an export control directive to suspend access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5.

items.title: Knicks face Spurs in Game 5, with first NBA title in 53 years in sight
items.summary: After an epic comeback, the New York Knicks head to San Antonio, hoping to beat the Spurs to seal the 2026 championship.

items.title: ‘Right to protest genocide’ denied: Scotland’s Palestine Action supporters
items.summary: As the banned group fights its proscription in London, similar battles are playing out in Edinburgh.

items.summary: US and Iran say a deal to end the war is close, with a final text agreed but key steps still pending.

items.title: Molokhia cigarettes: Gaza’s toxic alternative to tobacco
items.summary: Smokers in Gaza turn to toxic molokhia nicotine cigarettes as tobacco prices surge, causing serious health risks.

items.title: Doctors Without Borders investigation finds exploitation by staff in Chad
items.summary: The organisation employs tens of thousands of workers, local and foreign, to respond to crises across several countries.

items.summary: Brazil face Morocco as World Cup Day three brings predictions, key fixtures and other headlines.

items.title: Topuria shoves Gaethje by Lincoln Memorial ahead of White House UFC event

items.title: Separated by the Gaza war, a Palestinian yearns to see his family again
items.summary: Shady al-Areer, 38, was smuggled from Israel into the occupied West Bank after October 7, 2023. This is his story.

items.summary: The Israeli military has ordered residents of 20 Lebanese towns and villages to leave their homes immediately.

items.summary: Record five-time world champions Brazil open their 2026 campaign against African heavyweights Morocco on Saturday.
